skip_reason: statement_timeout=8s hit for BM25 path on structured/non-semantic lesson texts (delivery reports, task IDs, Russian task summaries) — these texts cause full BM25 index scan; excluded from overlap stats to avoid bias

corpus.source: random sample of active lessons with embeddings, excluding '[DOC:' and '[WATCHDOG]' prefixes and texts <80 chars (live corpus)

concerns_for_release_decision: overlap@10 p10 = 0.40 — worst 10% of queries have <40% overlap: for those queries recall_fast misses 60%+ of what recall_hybrid would find. Agent callers who depend on high recall for safety-critical lookups should use deep=True.
overlap@1 p10 = 0.00 — for some queries fast and hybrid agree on zero top-1 items: callers relying on top-1 for high-precision single-match (e.g. exact-fact retrieval) should use deep=True.
27% of sampled queries (8/30) timed out BM25 within 8s (structured/non-semantic lesson texts). overlap@K for those queries is unknown — could be lower than reported median.
Corpus sample is biased toward Russian-language task delivery summaries (majority of agent_lesson corpus in this DB). Real-world EN semantic lesson queries may show different overlap distribution.
methodology: overlap@K = |recall_fast top-K ∩ recall_hybrid top-K| / K measured over 22 completed queries (30 sampled, 8 skipped due to BM25 timeout) on live prod_corpus corpus (7612 lessons with embeddings). Queries are random active lesson embeddings reused as query vectors; lesson_text used as BM25 query for hybrid. recall_fast: HNSW cosine ORDER BY <=> LIMIT k only. recall_hybrid: HNSW + BM25 RRF (vec_weight=0.4, bm25_weight=0.4, rrf_k=60). No role/project filter (global corpus). statement_timeout=8s cap on hybrid. Benchmark script: benchmarks/scripts/bench_recall_fast_vs_hybrid_v010.py.
note: overlap@K = |recall_fast top-K ∩ recall_hybrid top-K| / K. Measures recall@K cost of dropping graph_walk + BM25 RRF (fast path). Threshold 0.70 = acceptable quality for interactive/MCP use-case. fast_latency < 200ms = interactive latency target for MCP default path.
